signature: Limit theorems and one-sided boundary crossings for DETERMINISTIC Birkhoff sums along a single orbit: temporal CLTs over rotations/odometers, "deterministic random walk" zeros/records, heavy points and one-sided discrepancy, and confined/oscillating orbits of x -> (3/2)x dynamics (the exact driving map of Antihydra's counter cocycle).

finds.ref: Kurt Mahler, "An unsolved problem on the powers of 3/2", 1968, J. Austral. Math. Soc. 8, 313-321 (reprinted Documenta Math. Extra Vol. Mahler Selecta 2019, 639-648)
finds.the_theorem: Call a>0 a Z-number if {a(3/2)^n} in [0,1/2) for ALL n>=0 (one-sided pointwise confinement, no other hypothesis). Theorem: there is at most one Z-number in each interval [g,g+1), and #{Z-numbers <= x} = O(x^0.7). Mechanism: confinement forces the integer parts to obey g_{n+1} = (3g_n + e_n)/2 with e_n = g_n mod 2 (= ceil(3g/2), the exact sibling of Antihydra's floor(3g/2)); the confinement forces the parity itinerary (e_n) to avoid the word 11, i.e. to live in the golden-mean subshift of entropy log phi, giving the count x^{log phi/log 2} = x^{0.694} <= x^{0.7}. Bonus device: the single series sum e_m (3/2)^m converges in R to 3r_0 AND 2-adically to -3g_0, so the itinerary determines the seed in both completions simultaneously.
finds.the_match: Verbatim our dynamics: Mahler's g-recursion is ceil(3n/2), Antihydra iterates floor(3n/2); both are the two branches-conventions of the same 2-to-1 Haar-preserving map on Z_2 whose parity itinerary is the Bernoulli(1/2,1/2) coding. Mahler's survivor set (orbits never crossing the r=1/2 boundary) is precisely a 'divergence-certificate'-type invariant set avoiding a boundary, and his golden-mean entropy phi is the SAME constant as the (1/phi)^n Antihydra halting heuristic (Lundberg root of r^3-2r^2+1=0). LOOSE PART, flagged: Mahler confines the real fractional part r_n pointwise at every step, while Antihydra constrains a cumulative Birkhoff sum (counter b_n > -1); pointwise confinement kills entropy immediately, a ballot-type sum constraint with positive drift does not (survival has positive Haar probability), so the counting bound transfers only to the WINDOW-CONFINED variant (b_n in [-1,C] forever), not to plain non-halting.
finds.transfer: Prove: (i) for the floor(3n/2) map, length-k parity words biject with residues mod 2^k (cylinder = arithmetic progression), hence for any subshift constraint L on itineraries, #{seeds n <= 2^k with itinerary in L} = #(L_k) = 2^{h(L)k/log 2}; (ii) apply with L = {counter stays in [-1,C]} (transfer-matrix entropy h(C) < log 2 since drift +1/2 must be cancelled) to get: #{n <= x : Antihydra counter stays in a bounded window for the first log2 x steps} <= x^{theta(C)}, theta(C) < 1 explicitly computable — a rigorous Mahler-style rarity theorem for critical (cryptid) behavior; (iii) port Mahler's real/2-adic double-convergence identity to characterize infinite survivors as a countable-or-dimension-theta set.

finds.ref: Leopold Flatto, Jeffrey C. Lagarias, Andrew D. Pollington, "On the range of fractional parts {xi(p/q)^n}", 1995, Acta Arithmetica 70.2, 125-147
finds.the_theorem: For ALL real xi > 0 and coprime p > q >= 2: limsup_n {xi(p/q)^n} - liminf_n {xi(p/q)^n} >= 1/p. In particular every orbit of x -> (3/2)x has fractional parts oscillating by >= 1/3. No measure, no Diophantine condition, no a.e. — every orbit. Proof is symbolic dynamics of the (p/q)-transformation: a combinatorial characterization of admissible itineraries excludes codes of orbits confined to a window shorter than 1/p. Companion results found alongside: Akiyama-Frougny-Sakarovitch (Israel J. Math 168, 2008, also Collatz-blind by full-text grep) CONSTRUCT nonzero x with ||x(3/2)^n|| < 1/3 for all n via their rational-base number system, and Dubickas (Math. Nachr. 2008) shows ||x(3/2)^n|| < 0.238117... for all n forces x=0; so the exact confinement threshold for x(3/2)^n is pinned in [0.2381..., 1/3].
finds.the_match: Exact dynamics: every Antihydra value orbit a_n = floor-iterates shadows a genuine real orbit xi(3/2)^n with bounded error (a_n(2/3)^n decreases to xi and a_n - xi(3/2)^n stays bounded), so FLP applies unconditionally to the shadow of the actual seed-8 orbit — the only every-orbit (not a.e.) theorem we found for this map. LOOSE PART, flagged: the theorem bounds pointwise oscillation of the fractional part, whereas Antihydra's halting functional is a cumulative parity sum; oscillation of {xi(3/2)^n} does not directly bound counter crossings. The transferable asset is the METHOD: an admissible-word characterization that excludes every-orbit confinement, which is exactly the proof-shape needed to exclude integer orbits with counter-confined itineraries.
finds.transfer: Adapt the FLP admissibility lemma from 'itinerary confined to an interval of length < 1/3' to 'itinerary whose ballot sum b_n stays in a bounded window': characterize which 0-1 words are realizable by INTEGER floor(3n/2)-orbits and prove an every-orbit lower bound on counter oscillation amplitude (e.g., limsup b_n - liminf b_n along recurrence scales), giving the first unconditional single-orbit statement about the Antihydra counter.

finds.ref: Dmitry Dolgopyat, Omri Sarig, "Temporal distributional limit theorems for dynamical systems", 2017, J. Stat. Phys. 166, 680-713
finds.the_theorem: Definitions: S_f(n,x) with n ~ Uniform{1..N}, x FIXED, satisfies a temporal DLT if (S-A_N)/B_N converges in law as N -> infinity. Theorem 4.1 (Beck's theorem, extended): for a rational, alpha a quadratic irrational, f_a = 1_[0,a) - a, EVERY x in T satisfies a temporal CLT: (S_a(n,x)-A_N)/(B sqrt(ln N)) -> N(0,1), B independent of x. Theorem 3.2 (the negative mirror): if the Birkhoff sums satisfy the ASIP w.r.t. mu (as they do for hyperbolic/expanding systems and iid-coded systems), then for mu-a.e. x the temporal distributions of S(n,x)/sqrt(T_n) realize EVERY probability law along subsequences — temporal statistics of a single orbit are a.s. lawless, and no temporal DLT can hold.
finds.the_match: Antihydra's counter b_n is literally S_f(n, seed) for f = +2 on evens, -1 on odds, over the floor(3x/2) map, which on (Z_2, Haar) is measure-isomorphic to the Bernoulli(1/2,1/2) shift (each branch scales Haar by |3/2|_2 = 2; parity itinerary = 2-adic digits of the seed). The centered cocycle satisfies the ASIP (iid steps), so Theorem 3.2 applies: for Haar-a.e. 2-adic seed the counter's temporal fluctuation laws are dense in all of P(R). LOOSE PART, flagged: 'a.e. seed' excludes nothing about the actual integer seed 8 (integers are Haar-null); and the positive result (Thm 4.1) needs a zero-entropy uniquely-ergodic driving rotation plus quadratic-irrational renormalization — floor(3x/2) has positive entropy and no known renormalization, so the Beck route to a fixed-seed CLT is provably closed, not merely unknown.
finds.transfer: Two provable statements: (a) the almost-sure (Gaussian) DLT and LIL for b_n w.r.t. Haar on Z_2 — hence for a.e. 2-adic seed, b_n = n/2 + O(sqrt(n log log n)) and the halting boundary is crossed at most finitely often (a.e. non-halting made precise); (b) a no-go theorem: no temporal distributional limit law exists for a.e. seed of Antihydra-type counters — any future 'limit law for the actual orbit' claim must exploit arithmetic of the specific seed, not ergodic genericity. This classifies which single-orbit statements are even possible.

finds.ref: Michael Bromberg, Corinna Ulcigrai, "A temporal central limit theorem for real-valued cocycles over rotations", 2018, Ann. Inst. H. Poincare Probab. Stat. 54(4), 2304-2334
finds.the_theorem: Let alpha be badly approximable and beta badly approximable with respect to alpha; f = 1_[0,beta) - beta over the rotation R_alpha. Then for ANY fixed initial point x, the temporal random variables (S_f(n,x) - A_N)/B_N (n ~ U{1..N}, B_N ~ sqrt(log N)) converge to a Gaussian. Mechanism: renormalization via continued fractions and a dynamical Ostrowski expansion of (alpha, beta, x). Extends Beck (alpha quadratic, beta rational, x=0) to Diophantine-generic parameters and all starting points.
finds.the_match: This is the state of the art for FIXED-orbit limit theorems for deterministic walks — precisely our wish for b_n. The hypotheses are the mismatch, fully flagged: (1) driving system is a compact-group rotation (zero entropy, uniquely ergodic) — floor(3x/2) on Z_2 is Bernoulli, positive entropy, continuum of invariant measures; (2) the proof engine is Ostrowski renormalization of the pair (alpha,beta) — no analogue exists for (3/2)-dynamics; (3) by Dolgopyat-Sarig Thm 3.2 the conclusion is provably FALSE for a.e. seed in our setting. The only bridge left open: the Akiyama-Frougny-Sakarovitch rational-base numeration gives an odometer-like successor structure adapted to (3/2)-dynamics; a temporal limit theorem for cocycles over the AFS '3/2-odometer' (a zero-entropy system!) is untouched territory and would be the correct analogue — note a temporal CLT already exists for multidimensional adding machines (arXiv 2001.00796), so odometer-type bases are provably capable of carrying such theorems.
finds.transfer: Formulate the AFS 3/2-numeration successor map as a dynamical system (it is zero-entropy, amenable to the odometer techniques), express a parity-type cocycle over it, and attempt the temporal CLT there; any success would give the first fixed-point limit theorem in the (3/2)-arithmetic world, with the Antihydra counter as a factor functional.

finds.ref: Artur Avila, Dmitry Dolgopyat, Eduard Duryev, Omri Sarig, "The visits to zero of a random walk driven by an irrational rotation", 2015, Israel J. Math. 207(2), 653-717 (with Aaronson-Keane, Proc. LMS 44 (1982) 535-553)
finds.the_theorem: For the deterministic walk S_n(x) = sum_{k<n} f(x+k alpha), f = 1_[1/2,1) - 1_[0,1/2), alpha quadratic irrational: precise asymptotics of the number of returns to zero N_n (Aaronson-Keane: c1 sqrt(n) ln n <= E(N_n) <= c2 sqrt(n) ln n for x uniform; ADDS give the distributional analysis via linear flows on the infinite staircase translation surface). Their own framing (verified in the paper, Collatz-absent): the SAME walk driven by the angle-DOUBLING map is exactly the simple random walk, since binary digits of Lebesgue-random x are iid.
finds.the_match: Boundary-visit counting for a deterministic Birkhoff walk — the ensemble analogue of counting Antihydra's near-halting events. Our driving map is measure-isomorphic to the doubling-map case they use as the 'chaotic' foil: under Haar on Z_2, Antihydra's counter IS a drifted simple random walk (+2/-1 fair coin). So the rotation results do not transfer (wrong driving system, and returns under drift are finite), but the paper's dichotomy sharpens exactly what our side of the dichotomy affords: SRW-with-drift local-time theory. LOOSE PART, flagged: everything on our side is a.e.-Haar; the integers are null, and unlike the rotation world there is no every-point theory to upgrade to.
finds.transfer: Compute exactly, for the Haar ensemble: distribution of the number of visits of b_n to the boundary level (geometric, ratio 1/phi via the Lundberg root), and prove that the exceptional set of 2-adic seeds whose counter returns to a bounded neighborhood of the boundary infinitely often has Hausdorff dimension log phi / log 2 = 0.694... in Z_2 — the SAME exponent as Mahler's x^0.7. A clean 'cryptids are dimension-0.694 rare' theorem unifying the two finds.

finds.ref: Henk Bruin, Robbert Fokkink, "On the records and zeros of a deterministic random walk", 2025, arXiv:2503.11734 (with Boshernitzan-Ralston one-sided discrepancy, Ralston arXiv:0906.4001 heaviness, Ying-Zheng arXiv:2210.08441)
finds.the_theorem: For the walk S_n(xi) with steps +-1 according to whether {k xi} < 1/2: the zeros and records of S_n(sqrt 2) and S_n(2 sqrt 2) are Pell-AUTOMATIC sequences (recognized by finite automata reading Pell-numeration representations; proofs partly via the Walnut automatic theorem prover), settling Kimberling's OEIS A120243 questions; they conjecture records are xi-Ostrowski-automatic for all quadratic xi. Load-bearing input: Boshernitzan-Ralston — the local discrepancy N(xi,I) - l(I)N is one-sidedly NONNEGATIVE for special intervals I = [0, h/k) tied to continued-fraction denominators (Ying-Zheng 2022 give the full iff characterization of one-sided boundedness for rational c); Ralston/Peres heaviness — in ergodic systems there exist (positive-measure sets of) points whose Birkhoff sums stay >= the mean for ALL n.
finds.the_match: The exact positive mirror of our negative result: we proved no regular (<= 7-state, base-2) DFA invariant set avoids 1 for the Collatz transduction; Bruin-Fokkink prove boundary sets (zeros/records) of rotation-driven deterministic walks ARE automatic — but in the numeration ADAPTED to the dynamics (Pell/Ostrowski), never base 2. Meanwhile AFS (Collatz-blind, grep-verified) prove the integer representations in base 3/2 form a NON-regular language which nonetheless supports letter-to-letter transducer arithmetic. LOOSE PART, flagged: automaticity of rotation walks flows from bounded partial quotients/self-similarity; there is no known self-similar structure for floor(3x/2), so this is heuristic guidance about the right representation, not a theorem to invoke.
finds.transfer: Redo our DFA/certificate search over AFS rational-base-3/2 representations instead of base 2: is the set of seeds whose Antihydra counter first hits the boundary at step k recognized (uniformly in k) by a finite transducer over AFS words? First concrete computation: implement the AFS letter-to-letter addition transducer, express one Antihydra step on AFS representations, and run the same bounded-state search that produced our base-2 non-regularity proof; either outcome (small automaton found, or non-regularity persists in the adapted numeration) is publishable structure.

finds.ref: Frank Aurzada, Thomas Simon, "Persistence probabilities and exponents", 2015, in Levy Matters V, Springer LNM 2149 (survey; classical Cramer-Lundberg theory therein)
finds.the_theorem: For an iid random walk S_n with positive drift and light tails, the ruin/one-sided-exit probability is P(inf_n S_n <= -b) = C r^{-b} (1+o(1)), where r > 1 is the adjustment (Lundberg) coefficient solving E[r^{-X}] = 1; persistence probabilities P(S_k > -b, k <= n) converge to the strictly positive survival probability. Hypotheses: genuine iid steps (or precise weak-dependence surrogates), fixed step law, ensemble probability.
finds.the_match: Under Haar measure on Z_2, the Antihydra parities ARE exactly iid fair coins (branch Jacobian computation, parity itinerary = 2-adic digits), so the theorem applies verbatim to the ensemble: with steps X in {+2,-1} equiprobable, E[r^{-X}] = 1 gives r^3 - 2r^2 + 1 = 0, i.e. r = phi — the BB community's (1/phi)^n halting heuristic becomes a THEOREM for the Haar ensemble: P(seed with counter offset b ever halts) = Theta(phi^{-b}). LOOSE PART, flagged in full: this is an ensemble statement; the Antihydra question is about the single integer seed 8, which is Haar-null, and no quenched/deterministic version exists for positive-entropy driving dynamics (Dolgopyat-Sarig Thm 3.2 shows single-orbit fluctuation laws are a.s. lawless).
finds.transfer: Write down, with proof, the exact ensemble theorem: 'for Haar-a.e. x in Z_2 the Antihydra counter diverges to +infinity; the halting set is an open subset of Z_2 of Haar measure p(b) = Theta(phi^{-b})' — plus the explicit generating-function computation of p via the Lundberg root, turning the folklore heuristic into the rigorous benchmark every certificate/automaton result should be measured against.

finds.ref: Franz Hofbauer, Gerhard Keller, "Ergodic properties of invariant measures for piecewise monotonic transformations", 1982, Math. Z. 180, 119-140 (with Renyi 1957, Parry 1960 for the beta = 3/2 acim)
finds.the_theorem: For piecewise monotonic expanding interval maps (beta-transformations T_beta(x) = beta x mod 1 included, any beta > 1, so beta = 3/2 qualifies) with their absolutely continuous invariant (Renyi-Parry) measure: the transfer operator on BV has a spectral gap, correlations of BV observables decay exponentially, and Birkhoff sums of BV observables satisfy the CLT (and, via Philipp-Stout/Melbourne-Nicol ASIP machinery, the LIL). Hypotheses: observable of bounded variation, initial point distributed according to the acim (equivalently Lebesgue-a.e. x).
finds.the_match: This answers the prompt's direct question: yes, cocycle limit theorems over the beta = 3/2 shift EXIST — CLT/ASIP/LIL for the digit observable 1_[2/3,1) and any BV parity-type function, w.r.t. Parry measure. So 'for Parry-a.e. real x, the parity-sum walk over T_{3/2} crosses any fixed one-sided boundary finitely often, with LIL-sized fluctuations' is rigorous. THREE gaps, flagged: (1) a.e.-real x, and integer-seed shadows form a null set; (2) the parity of the integer orbit floor(3n/2) is 2-adic data — it coincides with the real beta-digit itinerary only under Mahler-type confinement; the faithful compact model of the integer dynamics is (Z_2, Haar) (Bernoulli), with the beta-shift as the real projection of the same solenoidal extension; (3) whether beta = 3/2 is a beta-number (Parry admissibility sequence = the T_{3/2}-orbit of 1, eventually periodic?) is itself OPEN and equivalent to a powers-of-3/2 question, so even the symbolic space's finite description is conjectural.
finds.transfer: State and prove the solenoid version: the natural extension joining T_{3/2} on [0,1) (real expansion) with the 2-adic contraction is Bernoulli, and the Antihydra cocycle is a BV function of the real coordinate plus a locally constant function of the 2-adic coordinate; derive the joint CLT/LIL for the counter w.r.t. the solenoid measure — the sharpest 'generic seed' theorem available, and the precise statement of what remains: transferring from solenoid-a.e. to the single arithmetic orbit.

best_single_bet: The Mahler 1968 transfer (find 1). One week: Day 1-2, verify numerically and then prove the cylinder-residue bijection for floor(3n/2) (each length-k parity word is realized by exactly the seeds in one residue class mod 2^k — the analogue of Terras's theorem for this map, and the analogue of Mahler's unique-itinerary lemma). Day 3-4, transfer-matrix computation: entropy h(C) of the language of +2/-1 ballot words confined to the window [-1,C], giving the theorem #{seeds n <= x whose Antihydra counter stays in [-1,C] for the first log2 x steps} <= x^{h(C)/log 2} with h(C)/log 2 < 1 and -> log2(phi) = 0.694 as C -> 1 (Mahler's own exponent). Day 5-7, port Mahler's real/2-adic double-convergence identity (sum e_m (3/2)^m = 3r_0 in R and = -3g_0 in Q_2) to characterize infinite window-survivors, aiming at 'the set of seeds with forever-bounded counter is countable or has 2-adic Hausdorff dimension log2 phi'. This fences the cryptid rigorously — halting requires entering an explicitly dimension-0.694-rare critical regime — and every step is finite, checkable, and blind-source-grounded.
dead_ends: (1) Atkinson 1976 / K. Schmidt cocycle recurrence theory: hypotheses require ZERO-mean integrable cocycles over ergodic p.m.p. systems; Antihydra's cocycle has drift +1/2, so the theory only yields transience a.e. (weaker than the SLLN we already have) — nothing on exact boundary hitting. Do not re-search. (2) Temporal CLTs over positive-entropy driving systems: Dolgopyat-Sarig Thm 3.2 PROVES no temporal DLT can hold when ASIP holds (a.e. temporal lawlessness), so hunting Beck-type fixed-orbit CLTs over beta-shifts/Bernoulli systems is provably futile — the only open door is zero-entropy odometer-type systems (AFS numeration successor), recorded in find 4. (3) Rotor-router / Cooper-Doerr-Spencer-Tardos 'deterministic random walk': same phrase, different object (derandomized mass transport with rotor states); the O(1)-discrepancy theorems depend on rotor bookkeeping that has no analogue in parity-driven Birkhoff sums — discarded without download. (4) Unique ergodicity for floor(3x/2): none exists and none can — on Z_2 the map is Bernoulli with a continuum of invariant measures, on N there is no invariant probability; so every-orbit theorems must come from symbolic admissibility arguments (FLP-style), not from unique ergodicity — stop looking for uniquely-ergodic reformulations. (5) Heaviness (Peres/Ralston) as a direct tool: heavy points (Birkhoff sums >= mean forever) exist with positive Haar measure in our compact model, but for the drift-positive Antihydra cocycle this is weaker than SLLN, and for Collatz-proper (negative log-drift) heavy points for the mean-negative cocycle are exactly divergence candidates that heaviness theory cannot produce (it certifies >= mean, not >= 0 against the mean); kept only as framing for find 6. (6) Checked and confirmed keyword-blindness (zero grep hits for collatz/3x+1/syracuse in full texts) for Mahler 1968, FLP 1995, AFS 2008, Dolgopyat-Sarig 2017, Bromberg-Ulcigrai 2018, ADDS 2015, Bruin-Fokkink 2025 — but note the Collatz community is NOT blind in reverse: Lagarias's annotated bibliography lists Mahler's Z-numbers, so the Mahler link is known-but-unexploited rather than unknown; the specific transfer of his counting/entropy argument to counter-functionals appears genuinely undone.
